title: 帷幕
summary:
Following a conversation with Alton, the narrator observes him retrieving a potent barbiturate called Slumberwell from a small bathroom closet. Alton admits the drug is difficult to obtain without a prescription but claims to have a way around it. When the narrator asks if Alton knows Ezekiel, Alton becomes cold and defensive, instead pivoting the conversation to Ezra Pound, whom he describes as a drug addict. The narrator returns to his room, suspecting that Alton is "X" despite having openly voiced his suspicions.

At Styles Manor, the narrator observes that Hercule Poirot is physically weak and dependent on Curtis for mobility. The narrator resolves to act as Poirot's "eyes and ears." The narrator also visits Dr. Franklin’s laboratory, where Franklin conducts research on alkaloids extracted from "god's judgment beans." Franklin explains that these West African beans were used by tribes to distinguish the guilty from the innocent; he has discovered that one variety contains a bioalkaloid that counteracts the toxins of the other. During a discussion on morality, Franklin expresses a chilling view that 80% of humanity should be eradicated to improve the lives of the remaining population.

Seeking to test his suspicions regarding Alton, the narrator attempts to probe Judith's feelings toward him. However, the narrator's clumsy approach fails to elicit the desired information, as Judith reacts poorly to the questioning.
